revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24744680692|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Himesaka Kanachiana-jinja]][[ja:日咩坂鐘乳穴神社]] {{Expand Japanese|日咩坂鐘乳穴神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Himesaka Kanachiana Shrine | native_name = 日咩坂鍾乳穴神社 | image = Himesaka Kanachiana-jinja, naihaiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Himesaka Kanachiana Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.981675|133.604634}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Himesaka Kanachiana Shrine'''|日咩坂鍾乳穴神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Bitchū Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province|ja|備中国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11387310|1=List of Shikinaisha in Bitchū Province}} ** Series ordinal: 17 ** P155: {{ill|Q135041237|ltq=Q135041237|qid=Q135041237|1=Antsukino- Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135041238|ltq=Q135041238|qid=Q135041238|1=Widono- Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Niimi|lt=Niimi|ar|نيمي (أوكاياما)|azb|نی‌ایمی، اوکایاما|ceb|Niimi|ce|Ниими|de|Niimi|en|Niimi|envoyage|Niimi|es|Niimi (Okayama)|fa|نی‌ایمی، اوکایاما|fi|Niimi|fr|Niimi|gl|Niimi|id|Niimi, Okayama|it|Niimi|ja|新見市|javoyage|新見市|ko|니미시|mg|Niimi|mzn|نی‌ایمی، اوکایاما|nl|Niimi|pl|Niimi|pt|Niimi|ro|Niimi|ru|Ниими|sv|Niimi|tg|Ниими|th|นีมิ|tl|Niimi|tr|Niimi|tt|Ниими|uk|Ніїмі|vi|Niimi, Okayama|war|Niimi, Okayama|zh_classical|新見市|zh_min_nan|Niimi-chhī|zh_yue|新見|zh|新見市|zhvoyage|新見|qid=Q873596|1=Niimi}} * {{ill|Q1047514|ltq=Q1047514|qid=Q1047514|1=Bitchū Province}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.981675|133.604634}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Himesaka Kanachiana-jinja, naihaiden.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 183101 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * ひめさかかなちあなじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Himesaka Kanachiana-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '日本 岡山県新見市豊永赤馬6352番地', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '岡山県新見市豊永赤馬', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Himesaka Kanachiana-jinja}} [[JA:日咩坂鐘乳穴神社]] {{wikidata link|Q107297935}} == Japanese content == {{神社 | 名称 = Himesaka Kanachiana Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Himesaka_Kanachiana-jinja,_naihaiden.jpg|280px]]
Worship hall | 所在地 = {{JPN}} {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|qid=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Niimi|Q873596|qid=Q873596|1=Niimi}} Toyonaga Akama 6352 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} (formerly {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami|1=UNKNOWN}}) | 神体 = {{ill|lt=Himesaka Limestone Cave|Q107297830|qid=Q107297830|1=Himesakakanachiana Limestone Cave}} | 社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} | 創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|qid=Q1373210|1=Jinki (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=727|Q23557|qid=Q23557|1=AD 727}}) | 本殿 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} with {{ill|lt=karahafu|Q977293|qid=Q977293|1=Karahafu}} {{ill|lt=kōhai|Q11415970|qid=Q11415970|1=kōhai}} | 別名 = | 札所等 = | 例祭 = Autumn annual festival: {{ill|lt=November 11|Q2987|qid=Q2987|1=November 11}} | 神事 = Rice-planting festival: {{ill|lt=June 11|Q2617|qid=Q2617|1=June 11}}
Ohera-yaki offering festival: {{ill|lt=Old calendar|Q11511066|qid=Q11511066|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} {{ill|lt=January 11|Q2247|qid=Q2247|1=January 11}}, old-calendar November 11 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Okayama|label=Himesaka
Kanachiana Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Himesaka Kanachiana Shrine'''{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.okayama-jinjacho.or.jp/search/18930/|title=日咩坂鐘乳穴神社|website=Okayama Prefecture Shrine Office|accessdate=2021-06-28}} (ひめさかかなちあなじんじゃ; also 日咩坂鍾乳穴神社{{Sfn|鶴藤鹿忠|2007|p=48-49}}, 比賣坂鍾乳穴神社{{Sfn|加原耕作|1980|p=512-513}}{{Sfn|鶴藤鹿忠|2007|p=48-49}}) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Toyonaga Akama, {{ill|lt=Niimi|Q873596|qid=Q873596|1=Niimi}}, {{ill|lt=Okayama Prefecture|Q132936|qid=Q132936|1=Okayama Prefecture}}. It has as its {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=shintai|1=UNKNOWN}} the {{ill|lt=Himesaka Limestone Cave|Q107297830|qid=Q107297830|1=Himesakakanachiana Limestone Cave}}, called “Ōhorana (Great Cave)”{{Sfn|鶴藤鹿忠|2007|p=48-49}}. === History === According to the origin record of {{ill|lt=Sangō-ji|Q42579071|qid=Q42579071|1=Sangō-ji}} of the {{ill|lt=Shingon school|Q550182|qid=Q550182|1=Shingon Buddhism}}, which managed this shrine as its {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|qid=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}} until {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|qid=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}},{{Sfn|鶴藤鹿忠|2007|p=48-49}} in {{ill|lt=Jinki|Q1373210|qid=Q1373210|1=Jinki (era)}} 4 ({{ill|lt=727|Q23557|qid=Q23557|1=AD 727}}) {{ill|lt=Gyōki|Q1136664|qid=Q1136664|1=Gyōki}} visited this place and founded Sangō-ji, and when Kōbō Daishi ({{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|qid=Q222227|1=Kūkai}}) revived it in {{ill|lt=Daidō (Japan)|Q1373191|qid=Q1373191|lt=Daidō|1=Daidō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=802|Q29992|qid=Q29992|1=802}}), he invited {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami|1=UNKNOWN}} as guardian deities of the temple gate and enshrined them on the summit of Hongū-yama as '''Himesaka Shōnyūana Shrine'''{{Sfn|加原耕作|1980|p=512-513}}. Another theory says that even earlier, in {{ill|lt=Tenpyō-shōhō|Q1373169|qid=Q1373169|1=Tenpyō-shōhō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=705|Q23504|qid=Q23504|1=705}}), “Himesaka Daimyōjin” was enshrined at a place called Hongū on top of the great cave, while “Mio Takan-jō” was “Himesaka Myōjin”; Gyōki then merged them into “Himesaka Shōnyūana Shrine.” After that, 55 additional small shrines were merged{{Sfn|鶴藤鹿忠|2007|p=48-49}}. According to tradition, it was later {{ill|lt=transferred|Q10778681|qid=Q10778681|1=Kemerli}} from the summit to the current shrine site, and the shrine name was thereafter abbreviated as “Himemiya” and written as '''日咩宮'''. In Sangō-ji’s “Kōjōsho” of {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|qid=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 14 ({{ill|lt=1691|Q7682|qid=Q7682|1=1691}}) it appears as “日咩坂大明神,” on the plaque of the stone torii rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Kaei|Q1146125|qid=Q1146125|1=Kaei}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1849|Q7650|qid=Q7650|1=1849}}) as “日咩宮,” and on the plaque of the main sanctuary built in {{ill|lt=Bunka (era)|Q1373146|qid=Q1373146|lt=Bunka|1=Bunka}} 12 ({{ill|lt=1815|Q6948|qid=Q6948|1=1815}}) as “日咩坂神社”{{Sfn|加原耕作|1980|p=512-513}}. The small shrine still on the summit of Hongū-yama is called the inner shrine (okumiya), and many women unable to produce milk worshipped there as a deity of milk; this is said to have arisen from both the shrine name containing “乳 (milk)” and the {{ill|lt=stalagmites|Q181312|qid=Q181312|1=Stalagmite}} inside the cave resembling breasts{{SFn|加原耕作|1980|p=512-513}}. In the '{{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Register of Deities|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}' of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' enacted in {{ill|lt=Kōhō|Q1327182|qid=Q1327182|1=Kōhō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=967|Q23699|qid=Q23699|1=967}}), it is listed among 18 shrines of {{ill|lt=Bitchū Province|Q1047514|qid=Q1047514|lt=Bitchū|1=Bitchū Province}} as Himesaka Shōnyūana Shrine (Shikinaisha){{Sfn|鶴藤鹿忠|2007|p=48-49}}. It became a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} in Meiji 5, then in Meiji 43 ({{ill|lt=1910|Q2075|qid=Q2075|1=1910}}) merged Hachiman Shrine (village shrine) in Sabu and 32 unranked shrines, built up basic funds and expanded/rebuilt shrine buildings, and in {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1930|Q18787|qid=Q18787|1=1930}}) was promoted to {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=prefectural shrine|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{Sfn|加原耕作|1980|p=512-513}}. === Enshrined deities === As noted above, the shrine originally invited and enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi|1=UNKNOWN}} and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami|1=UNKNOWN}}, and this is also confirmed in the 1874 “Shikinaisha 22-sha Meisaichō.” However, a 1952 shrine details document lists principal deity {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōnamuchi-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} and accompanying deities {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kibitsuhiko-no-Mikoto|Q65318|qid=Q65318|1=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto}}, {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|lt=Ōtama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto|1=Ukanomitama}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ukemochi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Ōhirume-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kushiakaru-tama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Toyotama-hiko-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q1056017}}; it is considered that deity changes were made during the campaign for promotion to prefectural shrine{{Sfn|加原耕作|1980|p=512-513}}. === Rituals === The three main festivals are below. Two duty groups that handle annual festival arrangements and offerings are chosen from parishioners on {{ill|lt=March 3|Q2391|qid=Q2391|1=March 3}} (originally old-calendar 3rd month “uma” day). They are called Yuki-duty (spring duty) and Suki-duty (autumn duty){{SFn|加原耕作|1980|p=512-513}}{{SFn|鶴藤鹿忠|2007|p=48-49}}. When assigned duty, each builds a temporary altar in a clean place on their premises and invites Onzaki no Ōkami{{Efn2|Located at the back of the precincts, also called Oku-no-in. Common name: Ozaki Shrine{{Sfn|鶴藤鹿忠|2007|p=48-49}}.}}. In older times, those on duty also avoided roof replacement, attending funerals, and carrying impure things like {{ill|lt=compost|Q212254|qid=Q212254|1=Compost}}{{SFn|加原耕作|1980|p=512-513}}. === Autumn annual festival === The autumn annual festival is held on {{ill|lt=November 11|Q2987|qid=Q2987|1=November 11}} (originally old-calendar September 29). At this time, together with Hachiman Shrine in Shimoazae, {{ill|lt=Maniwa|Q861230|qid=Q861230|1=Maniwa}}, a mikoshi procession goes to “Aki-no-miya” in Kami-azae, which is thought related to the tradition that Himesaka Kanachiana connects to the “{{ill|lt=Suwa no Ana|Q11631895|qid=Q11631895|lt=Suwa Cave|1=Suwa Cave}}” in Shimoazae{{SFn|加原耕作|1980|p=512-513}}{{SFn|平凡社|1988}}. In older times, Aguchi Shrine in Aguchi, Maniwa, also joined this procession. On that day both shrines recite {{ill|lt=norito|Q1760119|qid=Q1760119|1=Norito}} together; after rites end, each returns while touring parishioner villages{{SFn|加原耕作|1980|p=512-513}}. === Rice-planting festival === The rice-planting festival has unclear origins but is said to have about 600 years of tradition; on {{ill|lt=November 8|Q2990|qid=Q2990|1=November 8}} of {{ill|lt=Shōwa|Q327567|qid=Q327567|1=Shōwa era}} 53 ({{ill|lt=1978|Q2483|qid=Q2483|1=1978}}), it was designated an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Niimi City{{SFn|鶴藤鹿忠|2007|p=48-49}}. It is held before the worship hall on June 11 (originally old-calendar ninth-month “uma” day){{Efn2|Tsurufu gives “old-calendar June 11,” and also notes duty transfer on old-calendar March 19, so there is inconsistency{{SFn|鶴藤鹿忠|2007|p=48-49}}.}}. After rites at the main shrine, a {{ill|lt=shimenawa|Q1887421|qid=Q1887421|1=Shimenawa}} is stretched in the precincts (formerly at the kagura hall), and that space is treated as a {{ill|lt=sacred rice field|Q10855080|qid=Q10855080|1=Shinden (divine field)}}{{SFn|加原耕作|1980|p=514-515}}. First, the Yuki and Suki duty groups each use a {{ill|lt=lion dance|Q713866|qid=Q713866|lt=lion head|1=Lion dance}} as an ox to pull a {{ill|lt=plow|Q216642|qid=Q216642|1=Spade}}, making a seedbed; the {{ill|lt=kannushi|Q15888735|qid=Q15888735|1=Kannushi (disambiguation)}} performs a dance and mime-sows seed rice. Next, cedar leaves are used as symbolic flute-like tools for the seedling-taking rite; then puddling is done again with the lions, followed by a rite called “rice planting,” miming transplanting with cedar leaves and throwing those leaves to parishioners waiting in front and back. Parishioners compete to collect them and take them home to place in fields for abundance{{SFn|加原耕作|1980|p=514-515}}. === Ohera-yaki offering festival === The Ohera-yaki offering festival preserves an old ritual form: the Suki duty performs it on old-calendar {{ill|lt=November 11|Q2987|qid=Q2987|1=November 11}} (originally old-calendar frost-month “uma” day), and the following year the Yuki duty performs it on old-calendar January 11 (originally old-calendar first-month “uma” day). Ohera-yaki is a mochi made by kneading {{ill|lt=rice flour|Q1269205|qid=Q1269205|1=Rice flour}} with {{ill|lt=amazake|Q455990|qid=Q455990|1=Amazake}}, mixing in split dried persimmons, and baking it on an iron {{ill|lt=spatula|Q164176|qid=Q164176|1=Niemodlin dialect}} (“ohera,” 25 cm long × 15 cm wide) used for ox-plowing. It is prepared the day before the festival and is said to have spiritual efficacy for driving away obstacles and healing illness{{SFn|平凡社|1988}}{{ill|lt=Mainichi Shimbun|Q1136866|qid=Q1136866|1=Mainichi Shimbun}}, Mar. 1, 2008, Okayama p.24, “Mini-news: ‘Ohera-yaki’ prayers for health in Niimi.”{{SFn|加原耕作|1980|p=514-515}}. For example, in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 20 ({{ill|lt=2008|Q2004|qid=Q2004|1=2008}}), when Mainichi covered it, 12 households in the Tochio district prepared Ohera-yaki using 40 kg rice, 25 kg amazake, and 240 dried persimmons. As customary, the mochi was offered at the shrine the next day for purification, then distributed to about 300 parishioner households. === Precinct images === The main sanctuary was built in Bunka 12 ({{ill|lt=1815|Q6948|qid=Q6948|1=1815}}), in a style common in the Bitchū area: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=irimoya-zukuri|1=UNKNOWN}} with {{ill|lt=karahafu|Q977293|qid=Q977293|1=Karahafu}} {{ill|lt=kōhai|Q11415970|qid=Q11415970|1=kōhai}}, originally {{ill|lt=hiwada-buki|Q90373477|qid=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} (recently reroofed with copper plate){{SFn|加原耕作|1980|p=514-515}}. The worship hall was rebuilt in {{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|qid=Q2430|1=1986}} (Shōwa 61) in reinforced concrete{{SFn|鶴藤鹿忠|2007|p=48-49}}{{Efn2|Before that, the worship hall was a 1909 ({{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|qid=Q2057|1=1909}}) four-sided eave-draped single-story wood-bark roof structure (later reroofed with tile){{SFn|加原耕作|1980|p=514-515}}.}}. The kagura hall dates to {{ill|lt=Hōei|Q1145262|qid=Q1145262|1=Hōei}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1707|Q6841|qid=Q6841|1=1707}}){{SFn|加原耕作|1980|p=514-515}}{{Efn2|Kagura hall: single-story thatched, frontage 6.5 ken, depth 2.5 ken. There is also a single-story shinsen-den with frontage 2 ken, depth 2 ken, wood-bark roof{{SFn|加原耕作|1980|p=514-515}}.}}. The Zuishin gate was built in Bunka 14 ({{ill|lt=1817|Q6958|qid=Q6958|1=1817}}), and the stone {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|qid=Q234731|1=Torii}} on the {{ill|lt=approach|Q667783|qid=Q667783|1=Sandō}} is the second generation rebuilt in Kaei 2 ({{ill|lt=1849|Q7650|qid=Q7650|1=1849}}){{SFn|加原耕作|1980|p=514-515}}{{Efn2|The first torii was built in {{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|qid=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 18 ({{ill|lt=1733|Q6972|qid=Q6972|1=1733}}) and was replaced after upper-part damage{{SFn|加原耕作|1980|p=514-515}}.}}. {{Gallery |title= |footer= |width=150 |height=170 |lines=2 |File:Himesaka Kanachiana-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii |File:Himesaka Kanachiana-jinja, zuishinmon.jpg|Zuishin gate |File:Himesaka Kanachiana-jinja, gehaiden-1.jpg|Kagura hall |File:Himesaka Kanachiana-jinja, gehaiden-2.jpg|Kagura hall (another angle) |File:Himesaka Kanachiana-jinja, shaden-1.jpg|Worship hall |File:Himesaka Kanachiana-jinja, shaden-2.jpg|Entire shrine buildings |File:Himesaka Kanachiana-jinja, honden.jpg|Main sanctuary section |File:Himesaka Kanachiana-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Precinct shrine }} === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist2}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{Cite book|ja|title=Report on Shikinaisha Survey Vol.22 Sanyōdō|publisher=Kōgakkan University Press|date=1980-2-20|page=509-515|ref={{SfnRef|加原耕作|1980}}|author=Kōsaku Kahara|editor=Shikinaisha Study Group|chapter=17 Himesaka Shōnyūana Shrine}} * {{Cite book|ja|title=Collected Survey Reports of Japanese Folk Performing Arts 16: Folk Performing Arts of Chūgoku Region 2 Hiroshima, Okayama|publisher=Kairo Shoin|date=2007-12-20|page=48-49|ref={{SfnRef|鶴藤鹿忠|2007}}|author=Shikachū Tsurufuji|editor=Okayama Board of Education / Hiroshima Board of Education|chapter=Rice-planting festival of Himesaka Shōnyūana Shrine}} * {{Cite book|ja|title=Place Names of Okayama Prefecture, Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei Vol.34|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|qid=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|date=1988-4-20|page=937|ref={{SfnRef|平凡社|1988}}|author=Heibonsha}} === Related items === {{Commonscat}}
